concerns.text: The plan equates the variance of the dynamic modularity time‑series with “flexibility” (US‑3 hypothesis) but provides no theoretical or empirical justification for this construct, risking a mismatch between the intended psychological construct and the measured metric.

Graph construction uses a fixed absolute correlation threshold of 0.2, yet the sensitivity analysis sweeps thresholds {0.01,0.05,0.1} and does not include the primary 0.2 value, creating an inconsistency and leaving the primary analysis untested for robustness.

Multiple‑comparison correction is described as applying FDR for “≥ 3 tests”, but the pipeline will generate at least five predictor‑outcome correlations (static modularity, segregation, dynamic mean, dynamic variance, flexibility). Limiting correction to a minimum of three tests may under‑control family‑wise error.

The CI‑time execution plan limits the run to 100 subjects, which is below the a‑priori power requirement of N≈194 (FR‑008). Although the plan flags the study as under‑powered, it still proceeds to produce correlation estimates, which could be misleading if presented without strong caveats.

Sliding‑window parameters (60 s window, 30 s step) are chosen without justification relative to the TR (0.72 s or 0.8 s) or the frequency band of interest, potentially compromising the reliability of the dynamic modularity metric.
